description_long: This is a square-seated, industrial-style stool with a distressed blue painted finish, revealing patches of underlying metal at the edges and corners. The stool features four straight, slightly splayed legs, each reinforced by two sets of horizontal support bars, one set near the base and another set higher up, serving as footrests. The seat is flat and unupholstered, with visible wear and chipping that gives it a rugged, urban character. The construction appears to be entirely metal, likely steel, providing both durability and a substantial weight. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative embellishments, emphasizing function over form. This stool would be well-suited for use in public spaces such as cafes, bus stops, or outdoor urban environments.
description: A distressed blue metal stool with a square seat, four legs, and visible wear, featuring horizontal support bars as footrests.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square seat, distressed blue paint, and two horizontal support bars connecting the legs.
description_view_1: Side view similar to the front, highlighting the wear on the paint and the alignment of the support bars.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, with visible chipping and the same structural features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing consistent wear patterns and the sturdy, industrial construction.
